title: AI-Enabled mm-Waveform Configuration for Autonomous Vehicles With Integrated Communication and Sensing
abstract: This paper develops a novel Integrated Communication and Sensing (ICS) framework established on the Markov decision process and deep reinforcement learning. The ICS-AV can adaptively optimize its waveform structure without requiring complete knowledge of the surrounding environment, maximizing sensing and data communication performance under dynamic and uncertain conditions. Simulations show up to 46.26% improvement in joint communication and sensing performance compared to baseline methods.
keywords: Internet of Vehicles (IoV) joint communication and sensing
problem: Separate communication and sensing systems in autonomous vehicles lead to increased hardware costs, complexity, and spectrum resource usage. Current vehicular communication standards (C-ITS at 5.9 GHz and DSRC at 5.9 GHz) have data rates (up to 27 Mbps) that don't meet AV requirements.
solution: An Integrated Communication and Sensing (ICS) framework based on Markov decision process and deep reinforcement learning to enable adaptive waveform structure optimization.
results: Up to 46.26% improvement in joint communication and sensing performance compared to baseline methods.
application_domain: Autonomous vehicles (self-driving cars, unmanned aerial vehicles) and Internet of Vehicles (IoV)
